channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.title: Bushes, 1st May
item.description:
Growing on parth making people walk in the road
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Over grown tree, 1st May
item.description:
The tree outside no 36 is now in full leaf. The weight of the leaves are now bowing it down. People pass have to either duck down or walk in the road which can be dangerous due to the speed of some cars. It is now over hanging the front garden and blocking out the light in the front room
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): The Chase, Widmore, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Rubbish dumped in stairwell fire exit, 1st May
item.description:
Please remove

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Lettsom Street, Camberwell, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Shoulder of mutton green welling, 1st May
item.description:
Repeated flying tipping of household refuse sacks. As of now there are a total of 4 bags dumped next to bins on the pathway Seems that it is becoming the norm to flytip on the pathway. Maybe covert cameras may catch those responsible ?

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Camborne Road, Falconwood, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Possible abandoned vehicle - see below, 1st May
item.description:
This car is parked on the left hand side of Southwood Road, opposite the junction with Woolf Close, just before the park gates. The tyres are starting to go flat and the car is gathering dust and dirt. It hasn't been moved since it arrived several weeks ago. I checked the DVLA site today and according to them the registration number is for a Silver Peugeot the tax on which expired on 31/08/2015. This clearly is not the same vehicle, which is LHD and has foreign registration plates.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Woolf Close, Thamesmead
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Bike not collected, reported 29/04, 1st May
item.description:
Forest Bike and Voi Bike reported on 29/04 had not been collected
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 135, Wapping High Street, Shadwell, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Large dip in the pavement. On the corner of the A40 Holborn and Furnival Street,, 1st May
item.description:
This large dip in the pavement - is outside the Wasabi restaurant. I have noticed it one month ago - but looks like it has been there longer.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Furnival Street, City of London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Footpath constantly obstructed by bikes, 1st May
item.description:
Ever since the student accommodation has opened, bicycles have been blocking the path. It’s forced people to walk around the blockage on to the road, which is terrible for mothers with prams, people with mobility issues, or elderly. There’s one bike in particular that’s chained to a parking sign pole - this one takes up the most space on the footpath and can’t be moved out of the way. Is there anything you can do to help?

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Weston Street, The Borough, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Vandalised sapling, 1st May
item.description:
2 trees planted last year, staked, with black water bags. the one on the right has not been vandalised

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Court Farm Road, Mottingham, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: This vehicle is not taxed and has not been moved for four months pluss, 1st May
item.description:
This vehicle Blue Peugeot 206 registration GF55FSO has no vehicle tax acording to the DVLA web site it has not moved from this position in The Greenway for four months could be stolen or abanderned
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 27, The Greenway, Ramsden, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Duck pond has dried up, 1st May
item.description:
The duck pond is drying up, again. Our water loving birds are losing their habitat. What is happening here? Why is pride not being taken in the upkeep of this beautiful and essential park? Is it safe to leave the pond like this? I fear that some people might think they can climb in. It’s already attracting litter. Please take better care of our natural spaces.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Tetty Way, Plaistow, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Piles of dumped junk 3 locations on Linden Grove, 1st May
item.description:
Dumped junk on Linden Grove. One pile including a mattress and other things at the junction of Water Mews and Linden Grove, a box of stuff dumped just to the left of the new builds on Linden Grove and a pile of old toys at the junction of Limes Walk and Linden Grove. Also a broken green Southwark bin full of rubbish on Linden Grove near the junction with Ivydale Rd. This means 4 lots of stuff to be removed from the south side of Linden Grove. There is also a pair of green leather armchairs and a leather foot stall left on Forrester Rd SE15 close to Linden Grove. The two armchairs and foot stool could possibly be suitable for a charity shop.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 1-3, Water Mews, Nunhead, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Park users being terrorised by 2 youths on motorbikes, 1st May
item.description:
There are two guys on unlicensed motor bikes who seem to be using the Recs as a dirt bike track with no consideration for anyone. They wear balaclavas. They have been spotted at different times of day but last Saturday and this afternoon were seen around 5 -5.30pm. I also saw one of them cutting through the Rec from Empress Drive into Whytes wood at around 7.30pm. On another occasion one was coming through the wood in the morning and said he’d just run me over if I didn’t move! They really are intimidating and drive so fast they terrify small children. Calling 101 doesn’t achieve anything. Can Wards Security Help please?
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Cow Path, Elmstead, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Fly Tipping on Street, 1st May
item.description:
Fly Tipping on Street
Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Evergreen Square, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: New N Rd x Popham Rd potholes, 1st May
item.description:
Multiple large cracks on the New North Road, Popham Road and Ecclesbourne Road intersection. Dangerous for cyclists and damaging for other road vehicles.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): New North Road, Islington, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Dog waste being washed out onto pavement from property and assortment of rubbish/litter outside property, 1st May
item.description:
Awful smell of waste coming from area and lots of litter and containers of rubbish outside property. This has been ongoing for well over a year.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Chatterton Road, Southborough, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Poor dead squirrel, 1st May
item.description:
:( poor dead squirrel
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Lubbock Road, Elmstead, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Large plywood panel on pavement, 1st May
item.description:
A large sheet of plywood which seems to be a panel from the inside of a van dumped on the pavement. Located on the left hand side of Southwood Road as you head towards the park gates currently leaning against the school railings..

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Woolf Close, Thamesmead
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Damaged road signs due to traffic incident, 1st May
item.description:
Unfortunately there was a traffic accident at the junction of Thornton Way/Wildwood Road and Meadway several months ago now, and the road signs that were hit, have yet to be fixed.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Thornton Way, Hampstead Garden Suburb, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: 12 Anson Rd, 1st May
item.description:
Pls collect these waste thabks

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Anson Road, London
Report on FixMyStreet
item.category: Fly-tip Large - More than a van load
